mass_measurements.reference: Yoon et al. 2010 (rotating model from interferometry)
Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating evolutionary fit to CHARA/MIRC)

radius_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (equatorial radius; rapid rotator)
radius_measurements.notes: Equatorial radius of an oblate near-pole-on rotator (i ≈ 4.7 deg, R_pole ≈ 2.36 R_sun, R_eq/R_pole ≈ 1.155). Mean radius collapsing the oblate figure ~2.55 R_sun. Phase 3 Decisions row uses R_eq because the pole-on viewer sees the equatorial disk projected.

radius_measurements.reference: Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA H-band; first 2D Vega resolution)
radius_measurements.notes: First-generation CHARA equatorial radius; superseded by higher-resolution Monnier 2012 imaging. Quoted 2.73 +/- 0.01 R_sun equatorial, 2.306 +/- 0.031 R_sun polar.

teff_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (CHARA/MIRC area-averaged from oblate model)
Yoon et al. 2010 (best-fit rotating non-LTE atmosphere)

luminosity_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (gravity-darkened model bolometric integral)

age_measurements.reference: Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating fit to CHARA/MIRC + Yoon-style isochrone)

metallicity_measurements.reference: Yoon et al. 2010 (best-fit photospheric [M/H] from rotating ATLAS9 fit)
Adelman 1988 (Reticon spectra; Fe abundance vs solar)
Qiu et al. 2001 (CCD high-res abundance pattern)

rotation_measurements.reference: Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA v_eq = 274 km/s) + Monnier 2012 R_eq
Petit et al. 2010 (NARVAL ZDI rotational modulation)

disk_measurements.morphology: IRAS 60 and 100 micron photometric excess; first 'Vega-like' debris disk detection
disk_measurements.notes: Discovery paper; no spatial resolution, geometry fields null

disk_measurements.morphology: first sub-mm resolved image of Vega's cold debris ring
disk_measurements.notes: 850 micron imaging; established cold ring structure but radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: OVRO 1.3 mm interferometric imaging; early sub-mm structure mapping of cold belt
disk_measurements.notes: No bibcode listed in Phase 3 bibliography; specific radii not adopted as canonical

disk_measurements.morphology: warm asteroid-analog inner belt at 14 AU; two-component SED + Spitzer-MIPS imaging
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical warm-belt fit (Spitzer-IRS + Herschel-PACS two-component SED)

disk_measurements.morphology: cold Kuiper-analog outer belt with inner edge at 62 AU; cleared gap from 14 to 62 AU implies planetesimal-stirring planets
disk_measurements.notes: Canonical cold-belt inner edge (two-component SED fit); outer edge taken from Sibthorpe 2010

disk_measurements.morphology: Herschel-PACS resolved imaging; cold belt extends to ~200 AU with relatively sharp outer edge, sharper inner edge than outer
disk_measurements.notes: 70/100/160 micron imaging; 70 AU inner-edge value (Su 2013 prefers 62 AU); outer-edge 200 AU is canonical for outer_radius_au

disk_measurements.morphology: SMA/early-ALMA 880 micron observations confirm cold-belt structure and constrain inner edge
disk_measurements.notes: Confirmation paper; specific radii not adopted as canonical

sources.title: Butkevich & Lindegren (2014): Relativistic aspects of stellar kinematics
SIMBAD Astronomical Database (Wenger et al. 2000)
used_for: astrometry (ra, dec, parallax, pmra, pmdec, rv)

sources.title: Yoon et al. 2010 (rotating model from interferometry)
Monnier et al. 2012 (rotating evolutionary fit to CHARA/MIRC)
Aufdenberg et al. 2006 (CHARA H-band; first 2D Vega resolution)

meta.notes: A0 Va main-sequence star, the modern archetype of a young near-pole-on rapid rotator viewed from a 0.45-Gyr-old evolutionary stage. Defining feature is the 2250 K pole-to-equator gradient resolved by CHARA: T_pole ~ 10150 K, T_eq ~ 7900 K (Aufdenberg 2006, Monnier 2012); the top-level teff_k = 9692 K is the SIMBAD-harmonized area-averaged photospheric value and is numerically interchangeable with Monnier 2012's recommended 9602 +/- 180 K within combined uncertainty. The pole/equator extremes are NOT recorded as separate teff_measurements entries because the schema expects a single area-averaged Teff per row; the gravity-darkening gradient is captured downstream in the Phase 3 Decisions field visual_gravity_darkening_pole_equator_temp_diff_k. Rotation: classical v sin i = 22 +/- 2 km/s (Gulliver 1994) is small because i is small (~6.2 deg), not because v_eq is small; true v_eq = 274 +/- 14 km/s (Aufdenberg 2006), placing Vega at omega/omega_crit ~ 0.91. The recommended rotation_measurements entry is the geometric P_eq = 2*pi*R_eq/v_eq ~ 0.521 d (12.5 h); Petit 2010 ZDI period 0.732 d reflects higher-latitude field-anchored structure in a differentially rotating gravity-darkened envelope and is kept as cross-check. Activity_measurements is intentionally empty: A0V has a radiative envelope with no convective dynamo, so the chromospheric log R'HK index is undefined (Ca II H&K lines appear purely as photospheric absorption with no core emission reversal). Robrade & Schmitt 2011 detected weak X-ray emission at log L_X ~ 25.5-26.0 erg/s, but the interpretation between a weak magnetic-pole-aligned corona (consistent with Petit 2010's polar field detection) and contamination from a low-mass companion is unresolved, so no x_ray entry is added pending interpretation. Metallicity: Vega is the prototype lambda Boo star, with photospheric [M/H] ~ -0.5 reflecting selective accretion of gas-depleted interstellar material rather than the bulk envelope composition — Yoon 2010 explicitly fits this as a photospheric layer and the bulk envelope is approximately solar; the cfg uses the photospheric value because it drives the visible SED. Mass: Yoon 2010 evolutionary 2.135 +/- 0.075 M_sun is consistent within 1-sigma with Monnier 2012's 2.15 +/- 0.10 M_sun from a different evolutionary code (recommended is Yoon as the lower formal uncertainty + canonical reference). Radius: equatorial 2.726 +/- 0.006 R_sun is from CHARA/MIRC (Monnier 2012); polar radius 2.36 R_sun and mean 2.55 R_sun are within the same paper; the cfg adopts equatorial because the pole-on viewer sees the equatorial disk. Age 0.455 +/- 0.013 Gyr (Yoon 2010) vs Monnier 2012's 0.7 +/- 0.075 Gyr — both rotating-isochrone fits with different boundary conditions; recommended is Yoon (newer paper for that specific evolutionary code; Monnier is preserved in Open items for future re-resolution).
